title: Bat Removal & Exclusion in Forest Hills, Queens | $900 – $2,500
metaDescription: Professional bat removal and exclusion services in Forest Hills, Queens. Humane, safe bat control for homes and businesses. Licensed experts. Call now for a free estimate!
intro: Forest Hills residents facing bat infestations need prompt, humane removal and exclusion services to protect their homes and families. Our licensed bat control specialists provide comprehensive solutions for properties throughout this historic Queens neighborhood, from Tudor-style homes near Forest Hills Gardens to multi-family buildings along Queens Boulevard. We ensure safe, legal bat removal with guaranteed exclusion to prevent future intrusions.
whyUs: Best Pest Control NYC has served Forest Hills homeowners for years with expert bat removal that complies with all New York wildlife protection laws. Our technicians are specially trained in humane exclusion methods and attic restoration, ensuring your property is bat-free with minimal disruption to your daily life.
localContext: Forest Hills' mature trees and classic early-20th-century architecture, including the charming Tudor and Colonial homes in Forest Hills Gardens, can provide ideal roosting spots for bats in attics, chimneys, and eaves. The neighborhood's proximity to Forest Park and abundant green spaces means bats are a common wildlife concern for homeowners seeking to protect their property values and family health.

faqs.question: How much does bat removal cost in Forest Hills?
faqs.answer: Bat removal and exclusion services in Forest Hills typically range from $900 to $2,500, depending on the size of the colony, entry points, and the extent of exclusion work needed. We provide free inspections and detailed estimates before any work begins.

faqs.question: Is bat removal legal in New York?
faqs.answer: Yes, but it must be done humanely and during specific times of year. New York law prohibits bat exclusion during maternity season (May 1 - August 15) when flightless young bats may be present. Our licensed professionals ensure all removal work complies with state and federal wildlife protection regulations.

faqs.question: What are signs of bats in my Forest Hills home?
faqs.answer: Common signs include squeaking or scratching sounds in attics or walls at dusk or dawn, droppings (guano) in attic spaces or near entry points, staining around potential entry holes, and seeing bats entering or exiting your home at twilight. A strong ammonia-like odor may also indicate a bat colony.

faqs.question: How do you prevent bats from returning?
faqs.answer: We use one-way exclusion devices that allow bats to exit but not re-enter, then seal all entry points with durable materials once all bats have left. We inspect and seal potential entries as small as 3/8 inch, including gaps in soffits, vents, chimneys, and roof edges. All exclusion work comes with a warranty.
